description: WHAT KIND of source Grossman et al. 2026 is, stated explicitly so that nothing downstream can quietly upgrade it. This is a NEW v2 method file, not an edit of nebpi_grossman2026_v1.json: that file is bound by hash into every release ever emitted, and editing it would make all of them unverifiable.

primary_source.title: Evaluating 'brain permeability': A critical issue for the development of therapeutic agents for primary and metastatic brain tumors
primary_source.authors_note: Stuart A. Grossman, Amy Barone, Roy E. Strowd, et al.
primary_source.article_content_hash_rule: sha256 over the PMC BioC response with the envelope's <date> element (the RETRIEVAL date, not the article) blanked. The raw bytes of an unchanged paper differ every day the endpoint is re-fetched; the content hash does not.

source_kind_detail: A peer-reviewed expert-consensus review arising from an Adult Brain Tumor Consortium (ABTC) / FDA workshop. Calling it the '2026 NEBPI framework' or 'ABTC/FDA workshop consensus' is accurate.
not_source_kind_statement: Grossman et al. 2026 is NOT an FDA guidance document, NOT a regulation, NOT a validated predictive model, and NOT a set of universal numeric BBB cutoffs. Calling it an FDA guideline would overstate its status. It is a criterion-level evidence framework: it tells you what evidence a permeability claim needs, not what number a drug scores.
consequences_for_stage4: NEBPI is a CRITERION-LEVEL evidence model, never a score. Stage 4 emits no NEBPI number.
An NEBPI class is a property of an evidence CONTEXT (active moiety x route x formulation x dose x schedule x population x tumour context), never of a drug.
The framework may not be cited as a regulatory requirement, and a drug that fails it has not failed an FDA standard.
Physicochemical descriptors and CNS-MPO are design benchmarks in this source, not proof of delivery, and cannot establish a permeability class alone or in combination.

cns_mpo_completeness_policy.statement: CNS-MPO may be INCOMPLETE, and an incomplete CNS-MPO never blocks, downgrades or gates a measured-evidence lane. Under the project's public-data-only rule a valid logD7.4 and most-basic pKa are frequently unavailable (PubChem computes neither; RDKit implements neither, and any 'RDKit ClogD' or 'RDKit pKa' would be fabricated). The correct outcome is status='incomplete' with total=null.
must_not: fabricate or impute logD7.4 or most-basic pKa
sum the available components into a partial total
make CNS-MPO completeness a prerequisite for the measured exposure, transporter, label-safety or NEBPI lanes
cns_mpo_completeness_policy.rationale: A compound whose sixth property is simply unknown would otherwise score lower than one whose sixth property is bad, and a descriptor gap would silently suppress real measured brain exposure. The descriptor lane is optional; the measured lanes are the evidence.
